title: STEM Pro Challenge
description: Take on 20 intermediate-level questions spanning various STEM disciplines to challenge your knowledge.

questions.questionText: What is the greatest common divisor (GCD) of 36 and 48?
questions.explanation: The GCD of 36 and 48 is 12, as it is the largest number that divides both.

questions.questionText: What is the remainder when 19² is divided by 6?
questions.explanation: 19² = 361. Dividing 361 by 6 gives a remainder of 1.

questions.questionText: A coin is tossed 3 times. What is the probability of getting exactly 2 heads?
If the mean of a data set is 10 and the sum of the data is 200, how many data points are there?
questions.explanation: Mean = Sum of data / Number of data points. Here, 10 = 200 / N, so N = 20.

questions.questionText: What does a firewall primarily do?
questions.explanation: A firewall protects a network by filtering unauthorized access.

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of two-factor authentication?
options: B) Increase security by requiring two forms of verification
questions.explanation: Two-factor authentication increases security by requiring two forms of verification.

questions.questionText: What is the inverse of the matrix [[1, 2], [3, 4]]?
questions.explanation: Using the formula for 2×2 matrix inverse: (1/det(A)) * adj(A), det(A) = -2, and adj(A) = [[4, -2], [-3, 1]].

questions.questionText: What is the trace of the matrix [[3, 5], [7, 2]]?
questions.explanation: The trace of a matrix is the sum of its diagonal elements. Trace = 3 + 7 = 10.

questions.questionText: Which thermodynamic process occurs at constant pressure?
questions.explanation: An isobaric process occurs at constant pressure.

questions.questionText: What is the efficiency of a Carnot engine operating between 400 K and 300 K?
questions.explanation: Efficiency = 1 - T₂/T₁. Substituting T₁ = 400 K and T₂ = 300 K: 1 - (300/400) = 0.25.

questions.questionText: Which type of galaxy is the Milky Way?
questions.explanation: The Milky Way is a barred spiral galaxy.

questions.questionText: What is the approximate age of the universe?
questions.explanation: The universe is approximately 13.8 billion years old according to current cosmological measurements.

questions.questionText: What is the output of the Python code: print(2 ** 3 ** 2)?
What does O(n log n) represent in computational complexity?
questions.explanation: O(n log n) is quasilinear complexity, common in efficient sorting algorithms like merge sort.

questions.questionText: Which gas contributes the most to the greenhouse effect?
questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide contributes the most to the greenhouse effect due to its high concentration.

options: A) Excess oxygen in water bodies
B) Excess nutrients leading to algae blooms
C) A decrease in water pH
questions.explanation: Eutrophication is caused by nutrient overloading, leading to excessive algae growth.

rewards.description: Earn 500 points for completing this challenge!
